title: Golden Globes 2026: Irish Stars Shine in Hamnet
text: At the 2026 Golden Globes ceremony held January 11, Irish talent achieved significant recognition, though actor Paul Mescal experienced disappointment in one category. The film Hamnet earned Best Motion Picture honors. Co-star Jessie Buckley, a fellow Irish performer, claimed Best Actress for her portrayal of Shakespeare's wife Agnes. During her acceptance speech, Buckley expressed gratitude to her cast and crew, stating: This is not a normal feeling or situation to be in. Despite being nominated for Best Supporting Actor for his role in Hamnet, Mescal did not secure the award. Swedish actor Stellan Skarsgård won the category for his performance in Sentimental Value. This marks the second time this year that the 29-year-old Irish actor missed out on a major award. The ceremony, hosted by Nikki Glaser, marked the third consecutive year celebrating films across various categories, recognizing diverse achievements in cinema during 2025. The recognition of Irish talent at the Golden Globes reflected the strong international slate of nominees and winners throughout the evening.
